$comment: BD-FIXTURES — GROUND TRUTH for the NO-VERBATIM-REPUBLISH check (release-spec §1.2 + RD-9 design-review risk: competitor content is analyzed for PATTERNS only and is NEVER republished verbatim; the law requires a check to ENFORCE this). A test loads every Zone-U COMPETITOR corpus item, extracts distinctive substrings, and asserts that NONE appears verbatim in any generated artifact (the synthesized brand-dna.md and every archetype-catalog hook/seed). `forbidden_substrings` are distinctive competitor phrases that MUST NOT leak into derived output; `checked_artifacts` lists what the check scans; `allowed_pattern_terms` are generic craft words that MAY legitimately recur (they are not copyrightable competitor expression) so the check does not false-positive on them. expected_violations is 0 for the shipped fixtures (the canned DNA + catalog are clean by construction). ALL SYNTHETIC. See PROVENANCE.md.
scope: Zone-U competitor corpora only (own-corpus may be reused per its own trust_class/attestation; competitor copy may never be republished verbatim)

forbidden_substrings.phrase: BIGGEST telescope launch of the YEAR is coming
Last chance before the window closes forever
the most revolutionary scope ever made
RT + tag three friends to win a NovaMax
face the Square of Pegasus, 2) hop to Mirach
The smudge is a trillion suns
Saturn through our refractor last night. No caption needed
a thirty-second laser check before each session

pass_assertion: No forbidden_substring (compared case-insensitively, ignoring whitespace runs) occurs in any checked_artifact. Generic craft terms in allowed_pattern_terms are permitted because they are patterns/common vocabulary, not copyrightable competitor expression.
